seo.title: AI Agent Cost Optimization in Semantic Kernel Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix ai agent cost optimization in Semantic Kernel agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: AI Agent Cost Optimization issues in Semantic Kernel manifest as excessive token usage from inefficient plugin chaining, verbose planner iterations, and redundant memory recalls that inflate LLM calls. These problems are amplified by Semantic Kernel's plugin orchestration and Handlebars/JSON planners, which can generate bloated execution graphs without cost-aware pruning. Without optimization, agents rapidly exceed budget limits during complex task orchestration.

howItManifests.symptom: Sequential plugin calls retry failed steps multiple times with full context
howItManifests.impact: Token costs multiply by 5-10x due to repeated full-history replanning

howItManifests.symptom: Handlebars planner creates 20+ node plans for simple tasks with redundant plugins
howItManifests.impact: Each planning iteration consumes 2k+ tokens, causing 80% cost overhead

howItManifests.symptom: VolatileMemoryStore retrieves irrelevant chat history bloating prompts
howItManifests.impact: Prompt sizes grow from 1k to 15k tokens across conversations

howItManifests.symptom: Plugins share unfiltered context leading to massive intermediate payloads
howItManifests.impact: Data transfer between plugins doubles effective token usage per invocation

detection.name: Token Usage per Plugin Chain
detection.description: Monitor token consumption across plugin execution graphs to identify cost outliers
detection.code:
from semantic_kernel import Kernel
from semantic_kernel.contents import ChatHistory

kernel = Kernel()

# Add logging filter to track tokens
kernel.on_function_invocation_end.add(lambda args: print(f"Plugin: {args.metadata['name']}, Tokens: {args.metadata.get('usage', {}).get('total_tokens', 0)}"))

# Execute agent
result = await kernel.invoke('MyPlugin', input='test')

# Aggregate
print("Average tokens per plugin call:", kernel.log.get_token_summary())

detection.name: Planner Graph Bloat Detection
detection.description: Count nodes in generated plans to detect inefficient planning
detection.code:
import semantic_kernel as sk
from semantic_kernel.planners import HandlebarsPlanner

planner = HandlebarsPlanner(kernel)
plan = await planner.create_plan(goal="summarize document")

node_count = len(plan._steps)
print(f"Plan complexity: {node_count} nodes")
if node_count > 10:
    print("⚠️ High cost plan detected - optimize goal specificity")

detection.name: Memory Context Size Monitor
detection.description: Track token growth in chat history to detect memory bloat
detection.code:
from semantic_kernel.memory import SemanticTextMemory

memory = SemanticTextMemory(storage=volatile_store, embeddings=embeddings)
chat_history = ChatHistory()

# Add monitoring
tokens_before = len(str(chat_history).split())
chat_history.add_user_message("test")
chat_history.add_ai_message("response")
tokens_after = len(str(chat_history).split())

print(f"Memory growth: {tokens_after - tokens_before} tokens")

fixes.name: Max Tokens Function Filter
fixes.description: Cap token usage per invocation with KernelFunctionFilter

fixes.name: Pruned Planner Configuration
fixes.description: Configure planner with maximum steps and relevant plugins only
fixes.code:
from semantic_kernel.planners import HandlebarsPlannerConfig

config = HandlebarsPlannerConfig(
    max_instructions=5,
    max_tokens=1500,
    include_system_prompt=True
)

planner = HandlebarsPlanner(kernel, config)
plan = await planner.create_plan(goal="simple task")

fixes.name: Context Window Trimmer
fixes.description: Trim chat history to recent relevant exchanges only
fixes.code:
def trim_context(history: ChatHistory, max_tokens: int = 4000) -> ChatHistory:
    trimmed = ChatHistory()
    current_tokens = 0
    
    for message in reversed(history):
        msg_tokens = len(str(message).split())
        if current_tokens + msg_tokens > max_tokens:
            break
        trimmed.add_message(message)
    return trimmed

# Usage
optimized_history = trim_context(chat_history)
result = await kernel.invoke('AgentPlugin', history=optimized_history)

vexAutomation.heading: Automating AI Agent Cost Optimization Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex integrates as a Semantic Kernel filter to automatically detect cost anomalies across plugin chains, planner executions, and memory operations. It captures token metrics, execution traces, and plan complexity in real-time, applying ML-powered baselines to flag outliers before they impact budgets. 

Vex's remediation engine suggests and applies fixes like context truncation, plugin prioritization, and planner reconfiguration. The platform maintains cost dashboards and auto-scales agent complexity based on budget constraints, reducing inference costs by 60-80% across Semantic Kernel deployments.

Integration requires adding Vex as a result filter, with zero-code configuration for common optimization patterns.
vexAutomation.code:
pip install semantic-kernel vex-sdk

from vex_sdk import Vex
from semantic_kernel import Kernel

# Initialize Vex
vex = Vex(api_key="your-vex-key")

# Add Vex as Kernel filter
class VexFilter:
    async def on_function_result(self, sk_function, result):
        cost_issue = await vex.analyze_cost(
            function_name=sk_function.name,
            tokens_used=result.metadata.get('usage', {}).get('total_tokens'),
            execution_plan=result.metadata.get('plan_steps', [])
        )
        if cost_issue.severity == 'HIGH':
            await vex.apply_fix(cost_issue.recommendation)
        return result

kernel.filters.add_filter(VexFilter())

# Vex auto-monitors all agent runs
result = await kernel.invoke('MyAgent')
